Amara Katabarwa, a quantum physicist from Georgia who works at Boston-based Zapata Computing Inc, one of QC's most innovative startups.

interaction_phrase: internship and later full-time job at Zapata
occurrence_sentence: There had been a problem I had been thinking about for a while: noise modeling. In quantum computing, one of the hardest things about quantum computers is understanding how to model the noise. This is a hard problem. You have to simplify your noise model in order to study how noise affects the computational process. One thing I was interested in was that there was some approximation of the noise that seemed to do very well.

interaction_phrase: internship and later full-time job at Zapata
occurrence_sentence: It was while I was working on my Ph.D., I came across a particular problem about quantum computing that fascinated me, and how I studied the problem that eventually led me to my internship and later full-time job at Zapata.

interaction_phrase: studied for a Ph.D. in Quantum Computing and Quantum Information theory at the University of Georgia
occurrence_sentence: TQD: You studied for a Ph.D. in Quantum Computing and Quantum Information theory at the University of Georgia, how was that experience? Did you learn a lot?
